nodes.label: B3.3 Muscle and Motility
nodes.description: The study of muscle structure, function, and the mechanisms of movement, including the detailed process of muscle contraction.

nodes.label: Excitation-Contraction Coupling
nodes.description: The physiological process linking the electrical excitation of the muscle cell membrane (sarcolemma) to the mechanical contraction of the muscle fiber.
nodes.pathwaySteps: Action Potential -> T-tubule Depolarization -> DHP Receptor Activation -> RyR Channel Opens -> Ca2+ Efflux into Sarcoplasm

nodes.label: Sliding Filament Theory
nodes.description: The mechanism by which muscle contraction occurs, involving the sliding of actin filaments past myosin filaments, resulting in sarcomere shortening.
nodes.pathwaySteps: Ca2+ binds to Troponin -> Tropomyosin shifts -> Myosin-binding sites exposed -> Myosin heads form cross-bridges -> Power Stroke -> ATP binds to release Myosin

nodes.label: Neuromuscular Junction
nodes.description: The specialized synapse between a motor neuron and a skeletal muscle fiber, where nerve impulses are transmitted to trigger muscle contraction.

nodes.label: Action Potential
nodes.description: An electrical signal (depolarization) that travels along the sarcolemma and T-tubules, initiating the excitation-contraction coupling process.

nodes.label: Acetylcholine
nodes.description: A neurotransmitter released by motor neurons at the neuromuscular junction; its binding to sarcolemmal receptors initiates depolarization.

nodes.label: Sarcolemma
nodes.description: The plasma membrane of a muscle fiber; it propagates the action potential into the muscle cell and contains receptors for acetylcholine.

nodes.label: Transverse Tubules (T-tubules)
nodes.description: Invaginations of the sarcolemma that extend deep into the muscle fiber, allowing rapid propagation of the action potential throughout the cell.

nodes.label: Dihydropyridine (DHP) Receptors
nodes.description: Voltage-sensitive receptors located on the T-tubule membrane; they undergo a conformational change upon depolarization and are mechanically coupled to ryanodine receptors.

nodes.label: Ryanodine Receptors (RyR)
nodes.description: Calcium release channels located on the sarcoplasmic reticulum membrane; mechanically activated by DHP receptors, allowing a massive efflux of calcium ions into the sarcoplasm.

nodes.label: Sarcoplasmic Reticulum (SR)
nodes.description: A specialized endoplasmic reticulum in muscle cells that stores and releases calcium ions, which are essential for muscle contraction.

nodes.label: Calcium Ions (Ca2+)
nodes.description: Key signaling molecules released from the SR into the sarcoplasm; they bind to troponin, initiating the molecular events of the sliding filament theory.

nodes.label: Sarcoplasm
nodes.description: The cytoplasm of a muscle fiber, where calcium ions are released and muscle contraction occurs.

nodes.label: Troponin
nodes.description: A protein complex on the actin filament that binds calcium ions, leading to a conformational change that shifts tropomyosin.

nodes.label: Tropomyosin
nodes.description: A filamentous protein that winds around actin filaments and, in a relaxed muscle, covers the myosin-binding sites, preventing myosin attachment.

nodes.label: Actin Filaments
nodes.description: Thin protein filaments in the sarcomere that contain myosin-binding sites; they are pulled by myosin during muscle contraction.

nodes.label: Myosin-binding Sites
nodes.description: Specific regions on the actin filaments where myosin heads attach to form cross-bridges.

nodes.label: Myosin Heads
nodes.description: Globular heads of the myosin protein that bind to actin, hydrolyze ATP, and perform the power stroke to pull actin filaments.

nodes.label: ATP Hydrolysis
nodes.description: The breakdown of ATP into ADP and inorganic phosphate, releasing energy that energizes myosin heads for cross-bridge formation.

nodes.label: Cross-bridges
nodes.description: The connections formed when energized myosin heads bind to the myosin-binding sites on actin filaments.

nodes.label: Power Stroke
nodes.description: The conformational change in the myosin head that pulls the actin filament towards the M-line, shortening the sarcomere.

nodes.label: M-line
nodes.description: The central line of the sarcomere, representing the anchoring point for myosin filaments; actin filaments are pulled towards it during contraction.

nodes.label: Sarcomere
nodes.description: The basic contractile unit of striated muscle, extending from one Z-disc to the next, composed of overlapping actin and myosin filaments.
